skill.name: teach-impeccable
skill.description: One-time setup that gathers design context for your project and saves it to your AI config file. Run once to establish persistent design guidelines.
skill.summary: Gathers design context through codebase exploration and targeted questions, then persists guidelines for consistent AI-assisted design decisions.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The skill is instructional markdown that guides AI to gather design context through codebase exploration and user questions. Markdown backticks were misidentified as shell commands. No cryptographic operations exist. Safe for publication.

content.user_title: Capture your design context for consistent AI collaboration
content.value_statement: AI assistants make better design decisions when they understand your brand and users. This skill runs once to gather project-specific design context and saves it for every future session.
actual_capabilities: Scans codebase for existing design patterns, documentation, and brand assets
Asks targeted UX questions about users, brand personality, and aesthetic preferences
Synthesizes findings into a structured Design Context section
Writes design guidelines to .impeccable.md or appends to your AI config file
Establishes persistent design principles that guide all future AI work
limitations: One-time setup skill - designed to run once per project, not repeatedly
Requires user interaction to answer UX-focused questions
Writes files to project root - requires filesystem write permissions
Does not generate visual designs or mockups, only textual guidelines

use_cases.title: Startup establishing brand identity
use_cases.description: A founding team uses this skill to capture their design vision early, ensuring all AI-generated UI components reflect their brand from day one.
use_cases.target_user: Startup founders and early engineering teams

use_cases.title: Agency onboarding client projects
use_cases.description: Design agencies run this once per client to encode brand guidelines, ensuring consistent output across multiple designers and developers using AI tools.

use_cases.title: Enterprise design system alignment
use_cases.description: Large teams use this to document existing design system constraints, helping AI assistants respect established patterns and accessibility requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Initial design context setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the teach-impeccable skill to gather design context for this project. Explore the codebase first, then ask me any UX questions that aren't already answered in the code.

prompt_templates.title: Refresh design context after rebrand
prompt_templates.prompt: I've updated our brand guidelines. Run teach-impeccable to refresh the Design Context section with our new direction and save it to both .impeccable.md and my AI config file.

prompt_templates.title: Add design context to existing config
prompt_templates.prompt: Run teach-impeccable to explore our codebase and capture design context. I want the output appended to my existing CLAUDE.md file so all team members benefit.

prompt_templates.title: Onboard new team member to design system
prompt_templates.prompt: Use teach-impeccable to document our design principles and guidelines. Then create a summary I can share with new team members joining the project.

output_examples.input: User runs teach-impeccable on a SaaS dashboard project
output: Design Context section written to .impeccable.md containing:
- Users: Product managers monitoring KPIs in time-constrained workflows
- Aesthetic Direction: Clean minimalism with purposeful color accents
- Design Principles: Clarity over density, Progressive disclosure, Consistent patterns

output_examples.input: User requests Design Context also be added to CLAUDE.md
output: Confirmation summary with key principles that will guide future work
Ready for consistent AI-assisted design across all sessions

best_practices: Run this skill early in the project lifecycle to establish design consistency from the start
Answer UX questions thoughtfully - the quality of output depends on the specificity of your input
Review and refine the generated Design Context before committing it to your repository
anti_patterns: Running this skill repeatedly - it is designed for one-time setup, not iterative refinement
Providing vague answers to UX questions like 'make it look good' without specific direction
Ignoring accessibility requirements - always specify WCAG level and user accommodations needed

faq.question: Do I need to run this skill every time I start a new session?
faq.answer: No. This is a one-time setup skill. Once it writes the Design Context to your config file, the AI will have access to those guidelines in every future session.

faq.question: What if my design guidelines change later?
faq.answer: You can re-run the skill to update the Design Context section. It will explore the codebase again and incorporate any new direction you provide.

faq.question: Does this skill require special permissions?
faq.answer: The skill reads your codebase and writes to .impeccable.md or your AI config file. No network access, external commands, or elevated permissions are required.

faq.question: Should I commit .impeccable.md to version control?
faq.answer: Yes. Design Context is project documentation that benefits the entire team. Commit it alongside other project documentation like README and CONTRIBUTING files.

faq.question: Can I use this with multiple config files like CLAUDE.md and .cursorrules?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill will ask if you want the Design Context appended to additional config files after writing to .impeccable.md.

faq.question: What if the skill misses important design context during exploration?
faq.answer: The skill asks you targeted questions about what it couldn't infer from the codebase. Provide any additional context during this Q&A phase to ensure completeness.
